ai.issueType: Menu toggle button does not update aria-expanded
ai.description: The click handler toggles the menu visibility using style.display but fails to update the aria-expanded attribute on the menuButton to reflect the open/closed state. Screen readers cannot announce the state change to users.

ai.issueType: Contact form validation does not set aria-invalid on fields
ai.description: When validation fails, error messages are created and appended to the DOM, but the invalid input fields do not receive aria-invalid="true". Screen readers cannot convey the invalid state to users.

ai.issueType: Contact form error messages not associated with inputs
ai.description: Error spans are created with unique IDs but are not programmatically associated with their respective input fields using aria-describedby. Screen readers cannot announce the error message when the field is focused.

ai.issueType: Load more button content update not announced
ai.description: New items are dynamically appended to the DOM after the fetch resolves, but there is no aria-live announcement to inform screen reader users that new content has loaded or how many items were added.
The cart count is incremented and the text content updated, but the change is not announced to screen readers. The code comment "// missing aria-live" acknowledges this gap.

The tab buttons have role="tab" but lack aria-controls attributes linking each button to its corresponding tabpanel. Screen readers cannot establish the relationship between tabs and their content panels.
Opening the modal does not move focus to it, and closing it does not restore focus to the trigger button. Keyboard users lose their place in the document. The modal also lacks aria-modal="true".
The email input in the newsletter form collects personal data but lacks the autocomplete="email" attribute, preventing browsers from autofill and violating SC 1.3.5.

ai.issueType: Contact form personal inputs missing autocomplete
ai.description: The contact form inputs (presumably including name, email, tel fields) lack autocomplete attributes (e.g., autocomplete="name", autocomplete="email", autocomplete="tel") to identify input purpose.

ai.issueType: Modal container not managed with aria-hidden
ai.description: The modal is shown and hidden by toggling style.display, but the script does not toggle aria-hidden on the modal container. When closed, the modal content may remain in the accessibility tree, causing confusion for screen reader users who might navigate to hidden content.

ai.issueType: Solar system canvas lacks keyboard interaction
ai.description: The solar system canvas has role="application" and handles mouse click/move events to select planets, but there is no keyboard event listener (keydown) implemented on the canvas to allow keyboard users to select or interact with the planets. Keyboard users are forced to use the list below instead of the main interactive surface.

lighthouse.issueType: Form elements do not have associated labels
lighthouse.description: Labels ensure that form controls are announced properly by assistive technologies, like screen readers. [Learn more about form element labels](https://dequeuniversity.com/rules/axe/4.11/label).

lighthouse.issueType: Form elements do not have associated labels
lighthouse.description: Labels ensure that form controls are announced properly by assistive technologies, like screen readers. [Learn more about form element labels](https://dequeuniversity.com/rules/axe/4.11/label).

lighthouse.issueType: Select elements do not have associated label elements.
lighthouse.description: Form elements without effective labels can create frustrating experiences for screen reader users. [Learn more about the `select` element](https://dequeuniversity.com/rules/axe/4.11/select-name).

lighthouse.issueType: Touch targets do not have sufficient size or spacing.
lighthouse.description: Touch targets with sufficient size and spacing help users who may have difficulty targeting small controls to activate the targets. [Learn more about touch targets](https://dequeuniversity.com/rules/axe/4.11/target-size).

lighthouse.issueType: Touch targets do not have sufficient size or spacing.
lighthouse.description: Touch targets with sufficient size and spacing help users who may have difficulty targeting small controls to activate the targets. [Learn more about touch targets](https://dequeuniversity.com/rules/axe/4.11/target-size).
